Effects: Search your deck for a Stadium card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

Name: Paldean Wooper

Name: Murkrow

Name: Honchkrow
Effects: Discard a card from your hand. If you can’t, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It is merciless by nature. It is said that it never forgives the mistakes of its Murkrow followers.

Name: Sneasel
Flavor-Text: This cunning Pokémon hides under the cover of darkness, waiting to attack its prey.

Name: Weavile
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from your Bench to the Active Spot, you may switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Basic Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: Evolution made it even more devious. It communicates by clawing signs in boulders.

Name: Tyranitar
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Discard the top 4 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: Extremely strong, it can change the landscape. It is so insolent that it doesn’t care about others.

Name: Sableye
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It dwells in the darkness of caves. It uses its sharp claws to dig up gems to nourish itself.

Name: Seviper
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It sharpens its swordlike tail on hard rocks. It hides in tall grass and strikes unwary prey with venomous fangs.

Name: Deino
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It can’t see, so its first approach to examining things is to bite them. You will be covered in wounds until a Deino warms up to you.

Name: Zweilous
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: The two heads do not get along at all. If you don’t give each head the same amount of attention, they’ll begin fighting out of jealousy.

Name: Hydreigon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 3 cards of your deck and attach any number of Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Discard the other cards.
Flavor-Text: Only the central head has a brain. It is very intelligent, but it thinks only of destruction.

Name: Maschiff
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It always scowls in an attempt to make opponents take it seriously, but even crying children will burst into laughter when they see Maschiff’s face.

Name: Maschiff

Name: Mabosstiff
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can store energy in its large dewlap. Mabosstiff unleashes this energy all at once to blow away enemies.

Name: Shroodle
Effects: Put a Basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Though usually a mellow Pokémon, it will sink its sharp, poison-soaked front teeth into any that anger it, causing paralysis in the object of its ire.

Name: Shroodle
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Though usually a mellow Pokémon, it will sink its sharp, poison-soaked front teeth into any that anger it, causing paralysis in the object of its ire.

Name: Grafaiai

Flavor-Text: The color of the poisonous saliva depends on what the Pokémon eats. Grafaiai covers its fingers in its saliva and draws patterns on trees in forests.

Name: Bombirdier
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It gathers things up in an apron made from shed feathers added to the Pokémon’s chest feathers, then drops those things from high places for fun.

Name: Corviknight
Effects: If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Corviknight can’t serve as a taxi service in Paldea because the Pokémon’s natural predators will attack it while it flies, endangering the customer.

Name: Cufant
Flavor-Text: Using the pointy tip of its trunk, it carves off chunks of hard rocks to eat. It is very docile and helps people with physical labor.

Name: Copperajah ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Flavor-Text: When attacked, this Pokémon will wield the tendrils on its body like fists and pelt the opponent with a storm of punches.

Name: Noibat
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon emits ultrasonic waves from its large ears to search for fruit to eat. It mistakes Applin for its food.

Name: Noivern ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Special Energy or Stadium cards from their hand.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Girafarig
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Though very small, the brain in its tail is still considered an important organ because it emits powerful psychic energy.

Name: Farigiraf
Effects: Choose a player. That player shuffles their hand into their deck and draws 4 cards.
Flavor-Text: Now that the brain waves from the head and tail are synced up, the psychic power of this Pokémon is 10 times stronger than Girafarig’s.

Name: Dunsparce
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It creates mazes in dark locations. When spotted, it flees into the ground by digging with its tail.

Name: Dudunsparce
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. Shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon uses its hard tail to make its nest by boring holes into bedrock deep underground. The nest can reach lengths of over six miles.

Name: Wingull
Flavor-Text: It soars high in the sky, riding on updrafts like a glider. It carries food tucked in its bill.

Name: Pelipper
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may choose 1: put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand; or search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, put it into your hand, and then shuffle your deck.

Name: Slakoth
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Vigoroth

Name: Fletchling

Name: Rookidee
Effects: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

Name: Corvisquire

Name: Therapeutic Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [C] Energy.The Pokémon this card is attached to recovers from being Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed and can’t be affected by those Special Conditions.

Name: Heracross
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It loves sweet nectar. To keep all the nectar to itself, it hurls rivals away with its prized horn.

Name: Tropius
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Move all Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Effects: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

Name: Fletchinder
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Fuecoco
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

Name: Crocalor

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

Name: Marill
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The fur on its body naturally repels water. It can stay dry even when it plays in the water.

Name: Eiscue
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Quaxly
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn’t happen.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon migrated to Paldea from distant lands long ago. The gel secreted by its feathers repels water and grime.

Name: Quaxwell
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon constantly run through shallow waters to train their legs, then compete with each other to see which of them kicks most gracefully.

Name: Frigibax
Flavor-Text: Frigibax absorbs heat through its dorsal fin and converts the heat into ice energy. The higher the temperature, the more energy Frigibax stores.

Name: Arctibax
Flavor-Text: Arctibax freezes the air around it, protecting its face with an ice mask and turning its dorsal fin into a blade of ice.

Name: Baxcalibur
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic [W] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon blasts cryogenic air out from its mouth. This air can instantly freeze even liquid-hot lava.

Name: Raichu
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [L] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

Name: Mismagius
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may move an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

Name: Gothorita
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon will hypnotize children to put them to sleep before carrying them away. Be wary of nights when the starlight is bright.

Name: Sandygast
Flavor-Text: If it loses its shovel, it will stick something else— like a branch—in its head to make do until it finds another shovel.

Name: Rabsca
Effects: Put a Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
